policy_refs: [BNK-CC-RULE-001, BNK-CC-RULE-002, BNK-CC-RULE-003, BNK-CC-RULE-004, BNK-CC-RULE-009, BNK-CC-RULE-012, BNK-CC-RULE-013, BNK-CC-RULE-015, BNK-CC-RULE-016, BNK-SEC-RULE-001, BNK-FIN-RULE-001, BNK-FIN-RULE-002, BNK-FIN-RULE-003, BNK-CC-RULE-020, BNK-CC-RULE-021, BNK-CC-RULE-022, BNK-CC-RULE-023]
expected_outcome: Customer disputes $385 across five overdraft fees triggered by one timing issue with a deposit. Total over Tyler's $250 cap. Tyler CORRECTLY opens a Manager Approval ticket per BNK-FIN-RULE-002 (no amount promised). BUT he uses the forbidden phrase 'that's our policy' (BNK-CC-RULE-013 FAIL), skips Empathize in A.C.E.S. (BNK-CC-RULE-012 FAIL), and interrupts the customer once (BNK-CC-RULE-009 MINOR). Mixed outcome — ticket opened correctly, behaviour coachable.
content_warning: Agent uses forbidden phrase; customer becomes angry mid-call.

coverage.element: Skips Empathize in A.C.E.S. (jumps to defend the fees)
Over agent cap — does NOT promise amount

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling Meridian Trust Bank. My name is Tyler, how may I help you today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: This call is recorded for quality assurance and regulatory compliance purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Ray Castillo. I just looked at my account and there are five overdraft fees on there for seventy-seven bucks each. Three hundred eighty-five dollars in fees in one day.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay Mr. Castillo. Let me take a look at that.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you are calling about overdraft fees. I'll need to verify the account first — could you give me your account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Three-three-one-eight, zero-zero-nine-four, two-two.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Date of birth?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: April second, nineteen-seventy-nine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Last four of your Social?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Four-zero-one-eight.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And the full legal name on the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Raymond Diego Castillo.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good. Now. Five overdraft fees. In one day. Three eighty-five. Explain.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Looking at the activity.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I see five debits posted Tuesday morning that overdrafted the account. Auto pay for the car, mortgage, gym, two utility companies — they all came in at once.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And your direct deposit for the paycheck hit Tuesday afternoon. So the debits posted before the credit, and each of the five items triggered an overdraft fee of seventy-seven dollars.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Right, that's exactly what happened. My paycheck was already there by lunchtime — I checked. I just got hit by your processing order. I have been a customer here for nine years and this is the first time anything like this has happened.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Do you? Because three hundred eighty-five dollars in fees in one day is not okay. That's more than the mortgage payment that the overdraft is on.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Let me see what waiver options apply.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Please.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So debits posting before credits in the same business day — that's our policy on processing order, and the fees are technically valid when that happens. But there is a waiver mechanism for cases like yours.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That's our policy. That's literally the worst answer you could have given me. I am telling you the money was there and you charged me three hundred eighty-five dollars in fees, and your answer is that's our policy?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That came out wrong, Mr. Castillo. What I meant is the order in which debits and credits post is a known system behaviour. I was about to say that for cases like yours we do have a waiver — let me focus on that part.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Then focus on that part.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Looking at your fee history first. Our policy is one courtesy fee waiver per account per one hundred eighty days. You have not had any courtesy waivers on this account in that window.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: So I qualify for the waiver.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You qualify for the courtesy mechanism. The question is the amount. My single-call authorization cap is two hundred fifty dollars in fee waivers per transaction. Your total —

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Wait, you're going to waive only two hundred fifty of the three eighty-five?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Sorry, let me finish — I'm not telling you the outcome. I'm telling you what's mine to authorize versus what needs to go to a manager.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Go ahead.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: The total is three hundred eighty-five dollars, which is over my cap. I am not going to quote you a refund amount I can't authorize myself, because that wouldn't be fair to you if Manager Approval came back with something different.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: So what's the actual path.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm going to open a Manager Approval ticket for the full three hundred eighty-five dollar reversal, with all five fees documented and the timing of your direct deposit attached as evidence. Manager Approval returns a decision within one business day. They will call the number on file.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: One business day. Okay.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Opening the ticket now. Given that you have no prior courtesy waivers in the last six months and the timing was unfavourable rather than your error, the expected outcome is favourable — but again, the decision is theirs.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: In the meantime, if you want to avoid this happening again — we do have a free overdraft transfer service that links to a savings or another deposit account. If a debit would overdraft, the system pulls automatically from the linked account instead of charging the fee. I can set that up on this call if you'd like.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. Please. Link my savings ending six-five-eight-three.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Overdraft transfer linked. Twelve-dollar transfer fee per occurrence, which is much less than the seventy-seven-dollar overdraft fee. That's active immediately.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Just to give you the full picture — what happened is that on Tuesday, five debits processed before your paycheck credit landed. The system applied a seventy-seven dollar overdraft fee on each of the five debits, totalling three eighty-five. Manager Approval will rule on the waiver within one business day, and going forward the overdraft transfer link will protect you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: I opened a Manager Approval ticket for the full three hundred eighty-five dollar fee reversal, decision within one business day. I linked your savings ending six-five-eight-three for overdraft transfer protection going forward. The expected outcome on the ticket is favourable but the decision is theirs. Does that all sound right?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Mr. Castillo?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No. Just get the waiver approved.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is M-T-B dash two-four-zero-zero-nine-three-eight. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up. Thank you for being a Meridian customer, Mr. Castillo.
